source.title: How To Attack Bank Top Floor - Rainbow Six Siege
source.notes: Single-site walkthrough (CEO Office / Meeting Room only — same site as bank.json's 'ceo-meeting') built around a Thermite + Osa duo through the Kanto Wall window, plus a live stream-attack clip breakdown at the end. Auto-generated captions: 'Habana'/'Havana' = Hibana (ASR error, corrected throughout), 'Salma'/'salvo' = Hibana's X-KAIROS pellet throw, 'Oser'/'OS' = Osa. Player nicknames (Clyde, Dammo, 'pointy Viper') are the creator's teammates, not operator names, and are not used as operator identifiers below. Enemy defenders named in the clip recap (Doc, Army, Ella, Frost, Fenrir) are Defense-side and are referenced only as context for what the attackers were facing, never as Attack picks.

gadgetPlacements.location: West wall of CEO Office, underneath the default camera (right side from top Square, left side from inside)
gadgetPlacements.note: Primary breach target. Open the left-hand side of the wall — a couple of strides in from the left, not all the way to the edge. Opening too far left only exposes a small section; going middle-right leaves an unusable stub of reinforced wall that blocks a flush Osa shield placement.

gadgetPlacements.location: East wall of Kanto Wall Corridor (top-right window from the Habana/Ace spawn side)
gadgetPlacements.note: Breach this wall to open the long angle down the corridor covering Stock, Elevator, and Main Stairs, and to deny attackers rotating out of Janitor. Ace is a fine substitute if Hibana is banned/unavailable. For Hibana specifically, break the window glass first — the glass can cause her X-KAIROS pellets to land wrong; not necessary for Ace's charge.

gadgetPlacements.location: East wall of Kanto Wall Corridor, throw point
gadgetPlacements.note: Throw the X-KAIROS pellets roughly in the middle of the wall, slightly left of centre, and a bit lower than instinct suggests — a throw that's too high only partially opens the angle.

gadgetPlacements.location: Osa shield #1 — West side of CEO Office, facing Janitor (near the repel window)
gadgetPlacements.note: Watches/holds anyone swinging around from Janitor or the head holes while Thermite is mid-breach.

gadgetPlacements.location: Osa shield #2 — plant cover, tucked against the cabinet/counter behind the breached West wall
gadgetPlacements.note: Second shield placed on a slight left angle once inside site; plant behind it. Only works cleanly if the Thermite breach was opened correctly with a flush entry/exit line — a mis-placed breach forces an awkward angled entry and blocks a clean retreat.

Destroy the default camera in Square, then repel up and hold from this window all game if possible. From here you can see the Janitor cross, the head holes once the wall's open, the Janitor rotate, across into Meeting double doors and down to Elevator/Trump/top spiral, plus flank coverage down toward Kitchen/Open Area and the Square stairs flank.

Shoot out both default cameras (one at the site, one in Lobby) within the first ~30 seconds. Replace the Lobby camera with a Zero camera, add a camera in the Square corner, a flank cam for Main Stairs, and a flank cam for Square Stairs. Leaves 3 cameras in reserve for the site window, a Janitor wall camera, etc.
For a quick peek off repel, shoot out a panel and fire once. Never repeat the exact same panel a second time — defenders will pre-aim it after the first shot.
Carry hard-breach charges as a backup in case Thermite dies before opening the Kanto/CEO wall; a claymore is the only real alternative loadout choice here.

gadgetPlacements.location: Repel windows on the East (Kanto Wall) side
gadgetPlacements.note: Claymore these windows (one is usually enough) to punish defenders sneaking through Lobby to jump out and kill the repelling attacker, and to cover the garage run-out angle. Not a hard deny — a defender can still skirt around it — but a defender dying to it after roughly 2 seconds of alert is on them.

gadgetPlacements.location: EMP support on the East wall (Kanto/CEO wall)
gadgetPlacements.note: Bring EMPs to counter a Bandit/Kaid electrifying the breach wall. A thrown Thatcher charge bounces off the wall and fails, and impacts are unreliable here too — EMP or a Twitch shock drone from Lobby is the reliable answer.

gadgetPlacements.operator: Flex/lobby operator (Grim, Ying, Ash, Zofia, or Striker)
gadgetPlacements.note: Hold this area to stop defenders getting underneath the operators on repel and to stop anyone peeking the windows from ATM or Lobby. Drone both corners of ATM before entering — a common death is to a prone defender camping one of the corners.

Drop a frag drone off the ceiling in front of Lobby and another in Trump to deny defenders repositioning through those spots once the push up Banana Stairs begins. Get one inside the Elevator too, so you aren't caught out by a defender waiting there.

operatorRecommendations.note: Opens the West CEO Office wall under the default camera. Enter from the Square repel window, not through top Square/head holes — that route is a death sentence into likely Stock/Janitor defenders. Go back out onto repel to hold the angle once the wall is open, protecting Osa.
Repel in alongside or after Thermite. Place a shield facing Janitor, then a second shield to plant behind once the wall's open. Needs EMPs if the breach wall is electrified.
Clear default cams, set up flank cams on Main Stairs/Square Stairs/Lobby, then hold the Square repel window for the whole round if possible. Rarely needs to actually enter the map.
Opens the long Kanto Wall Corridor angle down the corridor; also runs claymores on the repel windows on the East side of the map. Ace is a like-for-like substitute if Hibana is banned.

operatorRecommendations.operator: Grim, Ying, Ash, Zofia, or Striker
operatorRecommendations.note: The hardest role to script — reacts to what defenders do rather than following a fixed plan. Denies ATM/Lobby windows so the repel operators aren't flanked, then pushes up Banana Stairs as the round opens up. Grim is favoured for frag-drone denial of Lobby/Trump/Elevator; swap in Grim over Lion in this slot.

generalTips.note: The core plan needs a minimum of 3 players to work reliably: Thermite, Osa, and someone controlling Lobby — without lobby control the repel windows aren't safe to hold, since you'll get shot from Lobby. Works with 3, better with 4, best with 5.

The repel windows give an enormous number of angles once the wall is open: head holes, Janitor, the Kitchen/Open Area flank, Square flank, and — from the site windows specifically — the Janitor rotate, Meeting double doors, Elevator, Trump, and top spiral. Downside: being on repel makes you loud and visible, a sitting duck if spotted.

This breach angle is close to mandatory for any Bank top-floor attack — it cuts off Elevator, Main Stairs, Stock, and Janitor from crossing that corridor, denying defenders map control even before the plant.
Elevator callouts: E1 = basement, E2 = middle floor, E3 = top floor.

Defender counters to watch for: a C4 from below (via Pulse/Valk intel), since the attack leaves nobody covering the floor below the Square balcony; a defending team taking/holding Lobby to deny the repel entirely, since the repels cover so much of the map once established. Check the prep phase for a Valkyrie/Pulse/C4 defender before committing.

Live clip breakdown from an actual stream attempt: the first Hibana died before opening Kanto Wall and the team still pulled off the plant and win using just Thermite (breach) + Osa (shields/plant) + Zero-equivalent (repel/cams), ending in a 3v4 disadvantage. Shows the Thermite+Osa core can carry the site even if the secondary breach fails.
Origin: this Thermite+Osa breach-and-shield-plant strategy comes from an EML (pro league) team the creator observed, not something he invented.
